analysis.title: Detailed Analysis
analysis.summaryBuy: Most factors support buying (score {{score}}). Consider an entry with the risk management from the Trade Setup panel.
analysis.summarySell: Most factors are pressuring the price (score {{score}}). Avoid new long entries — consider taking profit or waiting for stabilization.
analysis.summaryHold: Bullish and bearish factors are still balanced (score {{score}}). Wait for stronger confirmation before entering.

factors.insufficientData: Not enough candle data ({{candles}} points) for a full analysis.
factors.rsiOversold: RSI {{rsi}} is in the oversold zone (<30) — selling pressure is overdone, bounce potential.
factors.rsiOverbought: RSI {{rsi}} is in the overbought zone (>70) — the rally is overheated, correction risk.
factors.rsiRecovering: RSI {{rsi}} is turning up from the lower area — selling pressure is easing.
factors.rsiWeakening: RSI {{rsi}} is turning down from the upper area — buying pressure is fading.
factors.rsiNeutral: RSI {{rsi}} is in the neutral zone — no extreme pressure either way.
factors.maGoldenCross: MA7 just crossed above MA25 (golden cross) — start of upward momentum.
factors.maDeathCross: MA7 just crossed below MA25 (death cross) — start of downward momentum.
factors.maBullish: MA7 is {{gap}}% above MA25 — short-term trend is up.
factors.maBearish: MA7 is {{gap}}% below MA25 — short-term trend is down.
factors.maFlat: MA7 and MA25 are overlapping — short-term trend is flat.
factors.priceAboveMa25: Price is {{gap}}% above MA25 — medium-term bias is positive.
factors.priceBelowMa25: Price is {{gap}}% below MA25 — medium-term bias is negative.
factors.priceAtMa25: Price is hugging MA25 — the market is undecided.
factors.macdBullishRising: MACD histogram is positive and expanding — buying momentum is building.
factors.macdBullishFading: MACD histogram is positive but shrinking — buying momentum is running out of steam.
factors.macdBearishFalling: MACD histogram is negative and expanding — selling momentum is building.
factors.macdBearishFading: MACD histogram is negative but narrowing — selling pressure is easing.
factors.momentumUp: Price rose {{roc}}% over the last 7 candles — short-term momentum is positive.
factors.momentumDown: Price moved {{roc}}% over the last 7 candles — short-term momentum is negative.
factors.momentumFlat: Price only moved {{roc}}% over the last 7 candles — momentum is flat.
factors.nearSupport: Price is only {{dist}}% above the 20-candle low — near support, limited downside room.
factors.nearResistance: Price is only {{dist}}% below the 20-candle high — near resistance, limited upside room.
factors.midRange: Price is mid-range over 20 candles ({{low}}% from support, {{high}}% to resistance) — far from both bounds.

tooltip.unrealizedGain: Unrealized profit or loss — the difference between current market value and total capital invested.
tooltip.cagr: Compound Annual Growth Rate — your portfolio's annualized growth rate based on historical data.
tooltip.rebalancingScore: A 0–100 score showing how well your asset allocation matches your risk profile target. Score ≥85 = optimal.
tooltip.avgCost: Average price per unit paid, accounting for all purchases and top-ups.
tooltip.capital: Total capital invested (buy price × units), before fees or taxes.
tooltip.realizedGain: Profit already realized from selling assets — locked in and will not change.
tooltip.staleAsset: This asset's price hasn't been updated this month. Update the price for accurate calculations.

projections.title: CAGR Simulator
projections.subtitle: Wealth accumulation projection based on portfolio data and monthly contributions.
projections.chartSubtitle: Based on current portfolio value + {{monthly}}/month contribution
projections.disclaimer: CAGR is an estimate — not a guarantee. Optimistic ({{optimistic}}%) and pessimistic ({{pessimistic}}%) scenarios show the range of possibilities based on historical asset class volatility.
projections.historicalBlendDesc: Rate is calculated from a blend of your historical portfolio performance over {{months}} months and the expected return model per asset class.
projections.allocationBasedDesc: Rate is calculated from the weighted average of expected return per asset class based on your current portfolio allocation.
projections.riskProfileBasedDesc: Not enough portfolio data to calculate CAGR from history or composition. Rate is taken from the {{profile}} risk profile benchmark.

help.title: User Guide
help.subtitle: Everything you need to know to get the most out of Trajectory.
help.glossaryDesc: Financial terms used throughout the app.
help.a_dashboard_1: Dashboard shows your portfolio summary: total value, unrealized gain/loss, estimated CAGR, and rebalancing score. Use the "vs IHSG" tab on the growth chart to compare your portfolio performance directly against the IHSG index.
help.a_dashboard_2: Unrealized gain/loss is the difference between the current market value of your assets and the total capital you've invested. It's called 'unrealized' because you haven't sold yet — the value changes as market prices fluctuate.
help.a_dashboard_3: CAGR (Compound Annual Growth Rate) is the estimated annualized compound growth rate of your portfolio. Calculated from portfolio history and/or current asset composition. This is a projection, not a guarantee.
help.a_dashboard_4: A 0–100 score showing how well your actual asset allocation matches your risk profile target. Score 85–100 = optimal, 60–84 = needs attention, below 60 = rebalancing needed soon.
help.q_portfolio_1: How do I add a new asset?
help.a_portfolio_1: Click 'New Position' on the Portfolio or Dashboard page. Select entry type 'New Position', fill in asset name, category, platform, buy price, and units. The asset projection will be calculated automatically.
help.q_portfolio_2: What is the difference between Top Up and New Position?
help.a_portfolio_2: New Position is used when buying an asset not yet in your portfolio. Top Up is used when adding units to an existing asset. Both will automatically update the average buy price (avg cost).
help.a_portfolio_3: An asset is marked stale if its price hasn't been updated in the current month. This matters because portfolio value calculations depend on current prices. Use a 'Price Update' entry to refresh it.
help.q_portfolio_4: How do I delete an asset?
help.a_portfolio_4: Open the asset detail, scroll down, click 'Delete Asset'. Warning: all related transactions (entries) will also be permanently deleted. Consider using 'Sell All' as an alternative if you want to keep the history.
help.q_rebalancing_1: How does the Rebalancing Audit work?
help.a_rebalancing_1: The system compares your actual asset allocation (percentage of each asset class) against the target allocation set in Settings. Differences above 3% will generate action recommendations.
help.q_rebalancing_2: How do I change the target allocation?
help.a_rebalancing_2: Open Settings, change your Risk Profile or Investment Horizon, then save. The target allocation will change according to the risk × horizon matrix. You can preview the changes before saving.
help.q_rebalancing_3: I've rebalanced but my score is still low, why?
help.a_rebalancing_3: Make sure all asset prices are up to date (no stale assets). The score is calculated from current market values, not invested capital. If any asset prices are stale, the allocation calculation won't be accurate.
help.q_projections_1: Where does the CAGR rate come from?
help.a_projections_1: The CAGR rate is calculated from three sources in priority order: (1) Blend of historical portfolio data × asset composition model if data ≥ 6 months, (2) Weighted average return model per asset class based on actual portfolio, (3) Risk profile benchmark as a last fallback.
help.q_projections_2: What are the Optimistic and Pessimistic scenarios?
help.a_projections_2: The Optimistic scenario assumes returns 1.5× the base rate (max 40%). The Pessimistic scenario assumes 0.5× the base rate (min 2%). These show a range of possibilities, not guarantees.
help.a_projections_3: This simulation is a planning tool, not a definite prediction. Markets can be far more volatile than assumptions. Use it as a guide for determining contributions and investment horizon, not as a guaranteed target.
help.q_chat_1: What can the Robo Advisor do?
help.a_chat_1: Robo Advisor is an AI with real-time access to your portfolio data. You can ask about your portfolio condition, request rebalancing recommendations, or ask the AI to change your risk profile and target allocation directly.
help.q_chat_2: Can the AI directly change my risk profile?
help.a_chat_2: Yes. If the AI recommends a change to your risk profile or target allocation, a confirmation card will appear. You can accept or reject the change before it's applied.
help.q_chat_3: Why can't I use Robo Advisor?
help.a_chat_3: Robo Advisor requires an API Key from an AI provider (OpenRouter, Groq, or OpenAI). Contact your administrator or see the setup documentation to configure VITE_AI_API_KEY in environment variables.
help.q_journal_1: What is recorded in the Journal?
help.a_journal_1: The Journal records all transaction entries: purchases, sales, top-ups, price updates, income (dividends/interest), fees, and corrections. This is an immutable log — every change is recorded as a new entry, not overwriting old data.
help.a_journal_2: A Correction is used to void an incorrect entry without deleting it. Corrected entries are marked and excluded from asset projection calculations, but the history is preserved for audit trail purposes.
help.q_settings_1: How does the Risk Profile affect the app?
help.a_settings_1: The Risk Profile determines the ideal target asset allocation (e.g. aggressive = more stocks/crypto, conservative = more bonds/cash). This target is used in the Rebalancing Audit and CAGR Simulator.
help.q_settings_2: What is the AI History feature?
help.a_settings_2: When enabled, your portfolio data (asset names, values, allocation, risk profile) is sent to the AI when using Robo Advisor. This data is used to provide more personalized and contextual recommendations.
help.q_settings_3: How do I change the app language?
help.a_settings_3: Open Settings, scroll to the 'Account' section, click the language toggle (ID/EN) to switch between Bahasa Indonesia and English. All labels, dates, and UI text will adjust instantly.
help.q_stocklive_1: How do I see real-time IDX stock prices?
help.a_stocklive_1: Open an IDX stock asset detail, then click the Market tab. The Market Price panel shows the latest price from Yahoo Finance (auto-refreshed every 90 seconds), change vs previous close, and a price chart (1D/5D/1M/1Y).
help.q_stocklive_2: What does the Sync price button do?
help.a_stocklive_2: The Sync button automatically creates a "Price Update" entry with the current market price and saves it to your ledger. This instantly updates unrealized gain/loss and portfolio value calculations without manual input.
help.q_stocklive_3: What is the difference between "Stored" and market price?
help.a_stocklive_3: The Stored price is the last price in your ledger (from the most recent entry). The Market price is the live price from Yahoo Finance. If they differ, the Stored label turns yellow as a reminder to sync.
help.q_forecast_1: How does the Next Close Estimate work?
help.a_forecast_1: The system runs 2,000 Monte Carlo simulations using a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model fitted to ~6 months of historical data. The result is a probability distribution of the next closing price, shown as median (P50), bear case (P10), and bull case (P90).
help.q_forecast_2: What are RSI, Bollinger Bands, and other technical signals?
help.a_forecast_2: RSI (Relative Strength Index) measures momentum — below 30 = oversold (potential rebound), above 70 = overbought (be cautious). Bollinger %B shows where the price sits relative to the volatility channel. 5D/20D Momentum is the actual return over the past 5 and 20 trading days.
help.a_forecast_3: These are statistical tools, not guaranteed predictions. The model uses zero drift (random walk assumption) for 1-day forecasts — meaning the median is always close to the last price, and the P10–P90 range reflects historical volatility. More volatile stocks produce wider ranges.
help.q_income_1: How do I track IDX stock dividends?
help.a_income_1: Open the Dividends & Coupons page, search for a stock ticker (BBCA, TLKM, etc.), then click + to add to your watchlist. The system shows 5-year dividend history, trailing 12-month yield, and payment consistency.
help.q_income_2: Where does the dividend data come from?
help.a_income_2: Dividend data is fetched in real-time from Yahoo Finance via a Vercel proxy. The system calculates trailing 12-month dividend yield (total dividends / current price × 100%) and shows per-event history.
help.a_income_3: Consistency is calculated from the number of unique years (out of the last 5) that had at least one dividend payment. A score of "5 out of 5 years" means the company paid dividends every year — a reliable indicator for dividend investors.
help.q_trading_1: What can I do on the Trading page?
help.a_trading_1: The Trading page provides a real-time crypto watchlist (via CoinGecko), a technical signal scanner (RSI + Moving Average), historical price charts per coin, and paper trading for buy/sell simulation without real money.
help.a_trading_2: Paper trading is a crypto trading simulation using virtual balance (not real money). Practice entry/exit strategies, view simulated P&L, and measure performance without risking capital.
help.q_trading_3: How do I read RSI signals in the scanner?
help.a_trading_3: The scanner shows 14-period RSI for each coin in your watchlist. RSI < 30 = oversold signal (potential entry), RSI > 70 = overbought signal (consider exiting or caution on entry). Combine with Moving Average trend for confirmation.
help.glossary_rsi: Relative Strength Index — overbought/oversold momentum indicator (0–100)
help.glossary_monte_carlo: Probabilistic simulation method using thousands of random scenarios
help.glossary_support_resistance: Historical price levels where price frequently reverses direction
help.glossary_yield: Dividend yield: total dividends ÷ share price × 100%

chat.title: Robo Advisor
chat.subtitle: AI powered by {{model}} · real-time
chat.noApiKeyDesc: Add VITE_AI_API_KEY, VITE_AI_API_URL, and VITE_AI_MODEL to .env then restart the dev server.

tour.welcomeDesc: This quick tour will guide you through all the main features of the platform. It takes about 2 minutes.
tour.dashboardStatsDesc: Here you can see your total portfolio value, unrealized gain/loss, and estimated CAGR at a glance.
tour.rebalancingScoreDesc: This score shows how balanced your portfolio is compared to your ideal allocation target. Click to see detailed recommendations.
tour.portfolioDesc: The Portfolio page displays all your assets. Add new positions, view details, and manage each asset easily.
tour.advisoryDesc: The Rebalancing page analyzes your portfolio composition vs target allocation and provides specific action recommendations.
tour.projectionsDesc: Project your portfolio's future growth based on historical CAGR and your monthly contribution targets.
tour.chatDesc: Ask the AI about your portfolio status, request rebalancing recommendations, or change your risk profile directly via chat.
tour.journalDesc: All transaction history is stored here — purchases, price updates, dividends, fees, and corrections.
tour.settingsDesc: Change your name, risk profile, allocation targets, language, and theme from the Settings page.
tour.helpDesc: Find complete documentation on every feature, including this tour, on the Guide page.
tour.finishDesc: You now know all of Trajectory's features. Start adding your first position, or replay this tour anytime from the Guide page.
tour.tradingDesc: Monitor real-time crypto prices, view RSI & Moving Average signals, and run paper trades — zero risk simulation.
tour.incomeDesc: Track IDX stock dividend schedules, add tickers to your watchlist, and see historical yield data directly from Yahoo Finance.
tour.stockLiveDesc: For IDX stocks, open the Market tab in asset detail to see real-time prices and intraday charts. Use the Sync button to write the market price to your ledger.

flow.title: Investor Flow
flow.subtitle: Technical estimate · not official IDX foreign/domestic data
flow.error: Data not available for this stock.
flow.disclaimer: Data is calculated from daily price & volume (Yahoo Finance). CMF, MFI, and OBV are technical proxies for institutional money flow — not official IDX foreign/domestic segment data. Real-time IDX data requires a paid service.
flow.tip_cmf:
Chaikin Money Flow (CMF)

Measures whether volume is higher when price rises (buying) or falls (selling) over 20 days.

> +15 = strong accumulation
−15 to +15 = neutral
< −15 = strong distribution
flow.tip_mfi:
Money Flow Index (MFI)

A volume-weighted RSI. Measures the speed and strength of money flowing in or out.

> 70 = overbought
< 30 = oversold
30–70 = normal
flow.tip_obv:
On-Balance Volume (OBV)

Adds volume on up days and subtracts on down days. A rising OBV trend means buying pressure is dominating cumulatively.
flow.tip_buypct:
Buy Pressure (%)

How close the closing price is to the day's high.

100% = closed at high (all buyers)
0% = closed at low (all sellers)
>55% = buy pressure dominant
flow.tip_netflow:
Net Money Flow

Estimated net buy/sell pressure per session, calculated from close position in day's range × volume × price.

Green bar = net buy
Red bar = net sell
Blue line = 10-session cumulative
flow.tip_signal:
Composite Signal

Calculated from 4 factors: CMF, MFI, OBV trend, and the ratio of accumulation vs distribution sessions in the last 10 days.

Signal strength is shown by the boxes next to the badge.
flow.tip_scorecard:
Daily Scorecard

Buy% = close position within day's range
CMF = CMF value for that day
▲ = accumulation session
▼ = distribution session
– = neutral
